text.title: use-case-modeling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for use-case-modeling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs write the happy path and stop, omitting alternative and exception flows.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force at least one alternative and one exception in the output schema; reject any output that omits them.

failure-mode.description: Drafter agents insert UI details ("displays popup") even after being told not to.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a post-generation regex check for UI nouns (button, modal, popup, screen, click) and bounce matching drafts.

failure-mode.description: Agents hallucinate actors that are UI elements (Header, Sidebar) rather than human or system roles.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Constrain actor type to {human, system, timer} in the output schema and reject anything else.

failure-mode.description: Drafting 10+ use cases in a single LLM call — accuracy collapses due to template repetition errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Chunk one UC per call; combine only at the index-generation step.

failure-mode.description: Feature as use case: "Display order screen" — no actor goal, no system behavior.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A use case must describe a goal an actor achieves, not a screen the system renders. Rename to "View Order Details" and add actor, flows, postconditions.

failure-mode.description: Overly abstract use case: "Use the system" — no specific goal, untestable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Abstract use cases cannot produce test cases; every use case must deliver a specific, measurable outcome for a named actor.

failure-mode.description: Main flow with 20 steps — two distinct actor goals bundled in one use case.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Split at the natural goal boundary. Apply the step-count rule: any main flow exceeding 9 steps is a splitting signal.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to writing the happy path and stopping.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always force ≥1 alternative + ≥1 exception flow in the schema; reject outputs that omit them.

failure-mode.description: Drafter agents invent UI details ("displays popup confirmation") even when told not to.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a post-generation regex check (button|modal|popup|screen|click) and bounce drafts that match.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the heuristic in the system prompt ("one step = one actor action + one system response") AND validate step count post-hoc.

failure-mode.description: Relationship inference (include, extend, generalization) is the highest-error part.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run only Opus, only after all UCs are drafted, and require rationale per edge.

failure-mode.description: LLMs hallucinate actors that are really UI elements ("Header", "Sidebar").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force actor type in {human, system, time} in the JSON schema; reject anything else.

failure-mode.description: Long context tempts "draft 30 UCs in one call".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Accuracy collapses past approximately 5 UCs per call due to template repetition errors. Chunk one UC per call.

failure-mode.description: Agents conflate primary and secondary actors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Make actor_type required and validate that exactly one primary actor is set per UC.

failure-mode.description: Reviewer agents grade their own drafts too generously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use a different model family (or at minimum a different system prompt with adversarial framing) for review than for drafting.

failure-mode.description: "Generate test cases from this UC" produces redundant tests when include is used.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Resolve includes inline before test generation, or de-duplicate by step ID.
